binascii/crc32.name: crc32
binascii/crc32.description: Compute CRC-32, the unsigned 32-bit checksum of data, starting with an initial CRC of value. The default initial CRC is zero. The algorithm is consistent with the ZIP file checksum. Since the algorithm is designed for use as a checksum algorithm, it is not suitable for use as a general hash algorithm. Use as follows: print(binascii.crc32(b"hello world")) # Or, in two pieces: crc = binascii.crc32(b"hello") crc = binascii.crc32(b" world", crc) print('crc32 = {:#010x}'.format(crc)) Changed in ve...

binascii/crc_hqx.name: crc_hqx
binascii/crc_hqx.description: Compute a 16-bit CRC value of data, starting with value as the initial CRC, and return the result. This uses the CRC-CCITT polynomial x16 + x12 + x5 + 1, often represented as 0x1021. This CRC is used in the binhex4 format.
